item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On this re-read I noticed that the first tester was a D class with initials JM.
The last tester was potentially a D class, now a Research Assistant, with last initial also M.
Was it possible the first and last testers were the same person?

But then I counted black boxes, 5 censored letters in one name and 7 in the other.

I think that two character edit would make for a nice, subtle, plausibly deniable, retconned easter egg.

item.title: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Image removed

item.title: Image source
item.content:encoded:
in October 2010, a bunch of people went on a club trip to Las Vegas. They gambled a bunch, and took pictures, and then one of them posted the pictures on her blog.

http://www.lasvegasdaze.com/2010/10/saturday-1023-neon-boneyard-and-meet.html

This picture is the very last one in the post, just before the comments. There is no indication that it is compatibly licensed with the SCP Foundation.

item.title: Locating the author
item.content:encoded: I am currently doing a youtube SCP Series (and i'm new to a lot of this forum stuff….plus the site is kinda slow for me) and i'm trying to get in touch with the author of this SCP to know if i can have permission to use his file for my first actual "SCP FILE" for my series. though not the flagship video for it. again, i'm super sorry if i shouldn't put this here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This kind of reminds me of that blackjack machine that can screw you over if you go into negative values while playing it.

Still a neat execution though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Has anyone done a tale about the person in the final log entry?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very much liked this one, once I figured out what it was going for. I do think that a few more logs wouldn't hurt, but it's fine as it is. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the best answer to your question is "Maybe". The followup question would be "How can you tell the difference?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's a retroactive reality warp and not an oracle?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will admit, it was the final log entry that got the upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exactly, if this is a reality altering device that can "free" d-class then the exact nature of the alteration needs to be determined. Otherwise what if the "freed" d-class history and records have been changed but the individual themselves still has the personality that led to becoming a d-class? It would be irresponsible of the Foundation to ignore any potential breach, regardless of how small the chances are, and in this case the chances seem large.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last note in the addendum really sold this for me, made the possibilities of this SCP's capabilities sort of mind boggling. +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hello there. There were originally more details on the internal construction, but I eventually cut that material out due to it being more verbose that it was interesting. The original intention was that the machine didn't function without all the "original" components, and some of them were tampered with to be non-functional. I justified the material being cut as these are more executive summaries rather than a complete technical writeup, and that part not being interesting except to a small audience.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's something going on here.

First, nothing is said about machine meters. Maybe they are of no interest, but still.
Second, there is a card reader on the picture and nothing is said about electronic fund transfer.
Third, a progressive jackpot presumes several machines grouped together, with a percentage of every bet on the group going to the jackpot fund. This machine is clearly standalone.
Fourth, bill and coin validators can be easily replaced.
Fifth, nothing is said about any slot machine interface board (SMIB) which is necessary for electronic fund transfer.

item.title: Re: Small Rewrite
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! I was initially worried due to the initial negative reception.

Causality is what I intended. The central question is whether the device is changing history or not, and from an IC perspective how can you even test if something has been changed. I might have used 'causation' instead, but I didn't like how the sentence sounded.

item.title: Re: Small Rewrite
item.content:encoded:
Did you mean to use "causalties" in the addendum?
Also, this has stuck around. Well done, you didn't think it could work.

item.title: Small Rewrite
item.content:encoded: I'm posting a small rewrite of the article to refocus it and cut out some superficial fluff. It should address what's been brought up so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's also possible that it was seized by the Nevada State Gaming Commission on orders from the Foundation, after they heard about those messages. (Especially if one of them were congratulating a player on the police never finding the evidence…)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No worries. Slot machines are one of the worst time sucks you can do. Also, someone complaining about one being rigged has already missed the point of the machines.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Much apreciated, I don't have much knowledge of slot machines and it was actually bugging me quite a bit.

This is a very plausible scenario, and I formally retract all quibbling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's how progressive jackpots work. They are constantly increasing and are actually shared between many machines, sometimes even between casinos. The actual credits on the machine are over on the right side.

Someone touched the machine and decided to play it thinking someone else must've had credits in it. When they went to do the payout they were rather upset at the message and complained to staff. The other people exposed before that just thought it was a cool joke on them.

Someone modifying a machine or using an unlicensed machine in public is very serious business and has to be investigated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm slowly doing a rewrite of a few sections to emphasize different aspects. That sentence was probably my least favorite in the article and was high on my list of things to tweak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That picture is messing with my head more than it should, the numbers just don't make sense. Does it increment the jackpot in pennies?

The only serious sticking point I have with this is the discovery. Why would people complain about a "rigged game", and why would the gaming commission seize the machine when it is physcially impossible to actually gamble with it? This machine can not accept money, so why wouldn't everyone involved just write it off as a goofy, free re-interpretation of a Zoltar machine?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconded, except that I edged into an up-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one. Small suggestion:

1895's anomalous effects manifest when it is touched by a living human being while it is powered and in an inactive state, and who has not activated its effects before.

Perhaps this might be better as "1895's anomalous effects manifest while it is powered and in an inactive state, then touched by a living human being who has not activated its effects before."

In any event, I enjoyed this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If this is doing what I think it's doing, then a definite +1 from me. If it isn't, but it has left enough vagueness to allow me to think that's what it's doing, then that gets the upvote too. Leaving conceptual spaces for imagination to fill in is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This hasn't done anything interesting for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The use of the pronoun "she" in the last paragraph makes the hook get lost a little. I'd use her name again with the blackout just to make certain your point is clear.

Over all, I'm not moved in any direction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The device may be altering history/reality, if so then there are certain ethical and security questions to ask about if you can really trust someone that used to be D-Class but was tweaked into a role at the Foundation. Until they can confirm what the device is actually doing, it's safer all the way around to err on the side of over-caution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"No record of D-█████ was located in Foundation records. She is currently being held per the zero tolerance escapee policy and all testing suspended until the full nature of SCP-1895's effects can be determined."

I may just be reading this wrong, but the way it's written it seems to say "D-whatever does not exist, but we've locked her up just in case".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article is ok, but I believe we already have an SCP slot machine. This is different enough from the other one I remember, so no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. I was planning on doing some edits to try to tighten up the writing tonight; I'll have to include your fixes after I find out for sure it was user error on my pass through for spell checking that let that all through.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Today, kids, we learn the importance of checking a dictionary.

THE PREFIX IS "DIS-", NOT "DISS-"!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No canon, you can use whatever grade you want ;) In my head canon, the grades differ for what they target, how they're prepared, and as well for how they are delivered.

The site used "Amnesiacs" until someone pointed out that amnestic was a word. It's split between which people prefer, but I'd never hold it against someone for preferring Class A Amnesiacs over using the right word Amnestics.

As for the removed AWCY? reference, I had the tag line hidden away into the final note since I viewed this as multiple levels of metaphors and the idea of someone complaining about a slot machine being rigged made me laugh. It was distracting to the effect I was going for, so I just dropped it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• It was a previous iteration.
  • You are completely wrong. Amnesiacs refers to people with amnesia, and the interpretations if the different classes of amnestics and what they do are up to personal interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: AWCY is a Group of Interest. It stands for Are We Cool Yet? The group tends to make strange objects for artistic purposes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Someone explain what the hell AWCY is for me? Maybe I'm just in a temporary state of derp.

The Foundation recovered the object and distributed class D amnestics to affected users and enforcement agents who had been exposed to SCP-1895.

That's amnesiacs. Also, those would be class A amnesiacs, the type used for short-term memory. I don't think the Foundation even has a class D amnesiac…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Writing needs tightening up (there's a few redundant phrases, for example). Otherwise, intriguing, not bad. I'll check back in a few days and see how it looks!

Edit: Well, it was more than a few days, but I like this. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not having seen the AWCY version, I actually liked this fine. Writing was a bit iffy at spots, and I'm not entirely sure what makes me like it, but I do, and that's the important part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would take it like a man but it's your thing so do what you want.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I went ahead and pulled that out, so he'd have to check the history to see it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It wasn't random, take another look at last payout message. It is, however, tacked-on and the straw that breaks my downvote threshold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I totally am, but early estimates seem to lean towards this not sticking around for long anyway. I'm just trying to buy Salman's upvote so I can have something to remember this by.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That kind of seems like you're chickening. Not that I would accuse you of anything, but some people may. Just sayin'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's eh. The seemingly random AWCY? tag didn't help, but keeping or removing tmit at this point wouldn't change my vote. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd say you're breaking my heart, but given the rate of AWCY hate in the first few minutes I'll leave this as something just found and remove the reference. Give me a minute or two to edit it out..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, I like this, but seriously FUCK ARE WE COOL YET?(!) I was really enjoying this and then you tacked on the reference at the end and it ruined the whole thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Throwing this in a collapsible
Cause I can

blah blah I'm a sell out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kinda iffy about this one. The AWCY? thing seems a bit forced, though.
